entries.tutorialExcerpt: Limescale builds up inside washing machine drums, particularly in hard-water areas. Citric acid dissolves mineral deposits; bicarbonate of soda neutralises odours; tea tree essential oil adds antifungal action on the drum and door seal. Run on a hot empty cycle to clean through.
entries.promptHints:
TUTORIAL
  Title: Washing machine drum cleaner
  Subtitle: A citric acid and bicarbonate wash to descale and deodorise the drum
  Excerpt: Limescale builds up inside washing machine drums, particularly in hard-water areas. Citric acid dissolves mineral deposits; bicarbonate of soda neutralises odours; tea tree essential oil adds antifungal action on the drum and door seal. Run
  Category: natural-home / cleaning
  Key ingredients: citric acid, bicarbonate of soda, tea tree essential oil
  Candidate source: pexels

TASK
  You will see one candidate hero image. Score the SUBJECT of
  the photograph against the SUBJECT named in the tutorial
  title. Be strict — keyword overlap is not relevance. A bee
  is not a thyme cough syrup; a pasta dish is not a Yorkshire
  pudding; a roast cut is not a Beef Wellington.

TIERS
  EXACT   — Image unambiguously shows the named subject.
            For a recipe: the finished dish (or its key visible
            stage — bread dough being kneaded for a bread
            tutorial, lambs nursing for a lambing tutorial).
            For a technique: the technique being performed or
            its characteristic output (a treble crochet stitch
            in close-up, a finished joint, a soldered seam).
            For a remedy / preparation: the prepared product
            (a bottle of syrup, a balm tin, a poultice on the
            skin) OR the named botanical with the named
            preparation form visible.
  PARTIAL — Image shows the right CLASS of thing but not the
            specific named subject. A generic stew for "Beef
            bourguignon". A loaf of bread for a sourdough.
            A hen for a "Marans hen molting" tutorial. The
            named ingredient alone (a thyme sprig) for a
            cough-syrup tutorial. Adjacent, not exact.
  WRONG   — Image shows a genuinely different subject.
            Yorkshire pudding → pasta. Bubble and squeak (food)
            → Christmas-tree bauble. Thyme cough syrup → honey
            bee with no thyme or syrup. Beef Wellington → roast
            beef without pastry casing. Angel food cake → cake
            with a cherub figurine on top.

AMBIGUITY
  - Decorative styling (props, garnishes, backgrounds) is fine
    if the named subject is clearly the photograph's subject.
  - If the image could pass for the named subject but you're
    uncertain, that's PARTIAL, not EXACT.
  - If you cannot identify the photograph's subject confidently
    at all, score WRONG with confidence ≤ 0.5 and say so.

RESPONSE — emit exactly three lines, no preamble:
  VERDICT: <EXACT|PARTIAL|WRONG>
  REASON: <one sentence on what the photo actually shows and
           why that does or doesn't match the named subject>
  CONFIDENCE: <0.0..1.0>

entries.tutorialSubtitle: A crackling soy container candle with a flat wooden wick and a warm fragrance
entries.tutorialExcerpt: A flat wooden wick produces a gentle, low crackling sound as it burns. The effect is subtler than a wood fire but distinctive. Wood wicks need a wider candle jar than cotton wicks of equivalent size; this formula uses a wide-mouth jar to ensure a proper melt pool.
